identity.name: the Kinder Sister
identity.story: Every kind word once bought her a diamond, and her sister's every unkind word bought a toad. She's kept the manners and let the jewels go. Now she keeps the town's common garden downwind of Reynard's orchard, on ground a fox's overdue honesty already softened, and swears the turnips come up truer for it.

relationships.kind: plants downwind of his orchard on purpose — the ground already knows how to behave

children.label: walking down to the borrowed ground
coaxing the turnips to come up honest
training the border hedge to hold its line, the way a kind word should

child.label: latching the garden gate for the night
leaving a kind word for the frost, just in case
